title: Anti-Dumping Duty
focus: Definition, mechanism of dumping, and GATT provisions.
rationale: A fundamental trade remedy concept frequently tested in UPSC for its definition and WTO/GATT legality.
sectionExcerpt: An anti-dumping duty is a protectionist tariff imposed on foreign imports dumped in the domestic market. Dumping occurs when a company exports a product at a significantly lower price than its domestic market price. The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) permits countries to take action against dumping.
evidenceBullets: Anti-dumping duty is a protectionist tariff against dumped foreign imports.
Dumping means exporting a product at a price significantly lower than its domestic market price.
GATT allows countries to take action against dumping.

title: Safeguard Duty
focus: WTO provisions, purpose, application rules, and duration limits of safeguard duties.
rationale: Safeguard duties have specific technical criteria (non-targeted principle, duration limits) that are highly examinable in UPSC prelims.
sectionExcerpt: Safeguard Duty is a WTO measure imposed to protect domestic players from a sudden and significant increase in imports. In principle, it cannot be targeted at a particular country, though individual country quotas apply under exceptional disproportionate import increases. It generally lasts up to four years, extendable to eight years.
evidenceBullets: Safeguard Duty is a measure available to WTO Member countries.
Imposed to provide a level-playing field against sudden and significant import increases.
In principle, safeguard measures cannot be targeted at a particular country.
Individual country quotas can be applied in exceptional circumstances of disproportionate import increases.
Duration is typically up to four years, extendable to a maximum of eight years.

title: Off-budget Borrowing
focus: Definition, mechanisms via PSUs, purpose, and macroeconomic concerns.
rationale: Off-budget borrowing is a critical fiscal policy concept related to transparency and fiscal deficit calculation, making it a prime target for economy questions.
sectionExcerpt: Off-budget borrowing refers to government borrowing not explicitly included in the official budget. It is typically raised through government-controlled PSUs or entities dependent on budgetary support. Its purpose is to keep borrowings off the fiscal deficit books, creating an impression of prudence, but it reduces transparency and conceals the true debt burden.
evidenceBullets: Off-budget borrowing is not explicitly included in the official budget or approved by the legislature.
Raised through government-controlled PSUs or entities dependent on budgetary support.
Keeps borrowings off the fiscal deficit books to create an impression of fiscal prudence.
Concerns include reduced transparency, lack of accountability, and concealment of the true debt burden.
